title: Hemigymnia arnottiana
intro: Hemigymnia arnottiana, also known as the "Fern Ally" or "Hairy Vines", is a type of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits, including reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of fever, due to its antipyretic and anti-inflammatory properties. Bioactive constituents of Hemigymnia arnottiana include alkaloids, terpenoids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of a tincture or ointment to treat various health conditions.
